Let me create a focused plan and then delegate Task 5 to OpenClaw. Since I can’t inspect the repo directly right now, I’ll assume standard Laravel 10/11 + Sanctum conventions.

*Plan: Complete Sprint 1 — Task 5 (Sanctum Authentication API)*

*Goal:* Implement a working token-based auth API using Laravel Sanctum.

*Assumed current state:*
- Laravel is installed.
- Users table/migration exists.
- Sanctum may or may not be installed/configured yet.

*Tasks to complete:*

*Task 5.1: Install & configure Sanctum*
- `composer require laravel/sanctum`
- Publish Sanctum config/migrations: `php artisan vendor:publish --provider="Laravel\Sanctum\SanctumServiceProvider"`
- Run migrations: `php artisan migrate`
- Add `HasApiTokens` trait to `App\Models\User`
- Ensure `sanctum` guard is in `config/auth.php`

*Task 5.2: Create API auth controllers*
- `RegisterController` — validate name/email/password, create user, issue token
- `LoginController` — validate credentials, issue token
- `LogoutController` — revoke current token
- `UserController` — return authenticated user

*Task 5.3: Wire up routes*
- In `routes/api.php`:
  - `POST /api/register`
  - `POST /api/login`
  - `POST /api/logout` (auth:sanctum)
  - `GET /api/user` (auth:sanctum)

*Task 5.4: Add tests*
- Feature tests for register, login, logout, and authenticated user fetch
- Run: `php artisan test`

*Task 5.5: Verify with curl/Postman*
- Register a user
- Login and capture token
- Hit `/api/user` with `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
- Logout and confirm token is revoked

To get `slack_post` (and other Slack messaging capabilities), you need to enable the `slack_messaging` toolset in your active Hermes profile.

Here are the likely fixes, from most to least common:

1. *Enable the Slack messaging toolset*
   ```bash
   hermes tools enable slack_messaging
   ```
   Then start a *new session* (`/reset` or restart the gateway) — tool changes don't take effect mid-conversation.

2. *Verify it's enabled*
   ```bash
   hermes tools list
   ```
   Look for `slack_messaging` (or `messaging`) and confirm it's on for your current platform/source.

3. *Check platform/source mapping*
   Your session source is `slack`. Some tools are gated by source/platform. Run:
   ```bash
   hermes config edit
   ```
   and look under `tools` or `platforms.slack` to make sure `slack_messaging` isn't disabled for Slack sessions.

4. *If the profile has a restricted toolset*
   You may have started this session with a limited toolset. Check how the session/gateway was launched and whether `-t` or a config override excluded messaging tools.

<@U0BCRBTEBGE> <@U0BDL9ZEGJE> <@U0BEA0PMU48> *# Agent Prompts — PulseDesk (Hermes + OpenClaw over Slack)*

Repo: <https://github.com/DevCadbury/forge2-chaman_kumar>

(e.g. `@Hermes ...`, `@OpenClaw ...`). Inside a thread the bot keeps replying without re-mention.

Tip for visible logs: keep each step in its own channel so the audit trail is obvious —
`#sprint-main` (you:left_right_arrow:Hermes), `#agent-coder` (Hermes→OpenClaw), `#agent-conv` (bots talking),
`#agent-log` (OpenClaw reports), `#ci-cd` (CI results), `#human-review` (your sign-off).

*## 1 · Kick off Hermes — paste in `#sprint-main`*

```
<@U0BCRBTEBGE> You are the product owner for PulseDesk (multi-tenant support-desk SaaS).
Repo: <https://github.com/DevCadbury/forge2-chaman_kumar> (clone it, branch main).

- README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, SUBMISSION.md
- implementation.md and guide.md (build + process plan)
- backend/ (Laravel 11 API) and frontend/ (React 19 SPA)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml

The Must + Should tiers are already implemented and 19 feature tests pass locally.
Your job now is a QA + hardening sprint, not a rebuild. Produce a tight Sprint 3 backlog of
small, scoped QA issues (max 5), post the plan here, then hand the first issue to <@U0BEA0PMU48> in
<#C0BDD2CCY1E|agent-coder>. Mirror the conversation into <#C0BEE1T9QE4|agent-conv>. Do not write code yourself.
Remember: humans merge to main after review in <#C0BEE2YJ1B2|human-review>.
```

*## 2 · QA handoff to OpenClaw — paste in `#agent-coder` (or let Hermes post it)*

```
<@U0BEA0PMU48> QA pass on PulseDesk. Repo: <https://github.com/DevCadbury/forge2-chaman_kumar>

Read first: implementation.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, backend/routes/api.php, and
backend/app/Models/Scopes/OrganizationScope.php so you understand the tenant model.

Then, on a branch `qa/sprint-03`:
1. Run the backend suite:  cd backend && php artisan test
2. Add/verify QA coverage for these risks and fix any real bug you find (root cause, do not
   weaken tests):
   - tenant isolation: a user from Org A gets 404 on Org B tickets/comments/activity
   - customers cannot read internal notes or set is_internal=true
   - customers cannot PATCH/assign/delete tickets (403), admins can delete
   - dashboard metrics + SLA breach math return sane values
   - notifications are created on assign + public reply, and only for the counterpart
3. Build the frontend:  cd frontend && npm install && npm run build
4. Open a PR against main. Do NOT merge.
5. Post your report to <#C0BCGLZBLES|agent-log> in this exact format:
   What I Did / What's Left / What Needs Your Call
   Include the test summary line (e.g. "Tests: N passed") and the PR link.
Mirror progress into <#C0BEE1T9QE4|agent-conv> as you go so the loop is visible.

<@U0BEA0PMU48> CI/CD task. Repo: <https://github.com/DevCadbury/forge2-chaman_kumar>
The workflow .github/workflows/ci.yml already runs backend tests on MySQL 8 + a frontend build.

tests pass. Approving and merging to main myself.
```
Then merge the PR on GitHub yourself (no bot merges to main).

---

*# CI/CD Guide — make build results visible in `#ci-cd`*

You have two layers of "CI/CD visibility". Do both for full marks.

*## A · GitHub Actions is your CI (already set up)*
`.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs on every push and PR:
- **backend** job: MySQL 8 service → `composer install` → `migrate` → `php artisan test`
- **frontend** job: `npm install` → `npm run build`

A green run on the repo's **Actions** tab is your CI evidence. Screenshot it into
`evidence/screenshots/05-ci-green.png`.

*## B · Pipe CI results into Slack `#ci-cd`*
Pick ONE of these. Option 1 is simplest and most reliable.

*### Option 1 — Slack Incoming Webhook (recommended)*
1. In Slack: create an **Incoming Webhook** for the `#ci-cd` channel
   (<http://api.slack.com/apps|api.slack.com/apps> → your app → Incoming Webhooks → Add New Webhook to Workspace → pick
   `#ci-cd`). Copy the `<https://hooks.slack.com/services/>...` URL.
2. In GitHub: repo → **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret**
   - Name: `SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL`
   - Value: the webhook URL
3. Add a notify step to **both** jobs in `ci.yml` (runs even on failure):

```yaml
      - name: Notify Slack
        if: always()
        run: |
          curl -s -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' \
            --data "{\"text\":\"CI ${{ job.status }} · ${{ github.workflow }} · ${{ github.ref_name }} · ${{ github.event_head_commit.message || github.sha }} · ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}" \
            ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
```

_> Put it as the last step of the `backend` and `frontend` jobs so each posts its own status._

*### Option 2 — a Slack GitHub Action*
Use a marketplace action (e.g. `slackapi/slack-github-action`) with the same
`SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL` secret. Functionally equivalent; Option 1 needs no extra dependency.

*### Option 3 — agent-posted CI (counts too)*
Have the CI/CD OpenClaw paste the test output + Actions run URL into `#ci-cd` after each run.
This is the lowest-tech path and still produces a visible trail, but the webhook (Option 1) is
automatic and looks cleaner to judges.

*## C · The loop that produces a clean audit trail*
For each QA issue:
1. `#sprint-main` — you give Hermes the goal; Hermes posts the plan.
2. `#agent-coder` — Hermes hands the issue to OpenClaw.
3. `#agent-conv` — the two bots converse as work happens.
4. OpenClaw opens a PR → GitHub Actions runs → **#ci-cd** gets the result (webhook).
5. `#agent-log` — OpenClaw posts What I Did / What's Left / What Needs Your Call + PR link.
6. `#human-review` — you approve, then **you merge** on GitHub.
7. Repeat. Then export Slack into `slack-export/` and screenshot the green CI + app.

*## D · Quick checklist before you submit*
- [ ] At least one green Actions run (backend + frontend)
- [ ] `#ci-cd` shows CI results (webhook messages or agent posts)
- [ ] `#agent-log` has real OpenClaw reports with PR links
- [ ] `#human-review` shows you approving before each merge
- [ ] Slack exported to `slack-export/`; CI + app screenshots in `evidence/screenshots/`
